theme_notes: Taglines draw from FIFA being subpoenaed over $380–$33,000 World Cup 2026 ticket prices (NY/NJ AGs investigating, tournament two weeks out), the US-Iran draft peace deal stalling because Trump and Khamenei won't sign, a former senior CIA official caught storing $40M in stolen gold bars at his home, and Ebola healthcare workers evacuated at gunpoint in Congo's Ituri province.
settled: Even-steven. FIFA couldn't price-gouge you here even if they tried.
Cleared to zero. Unlike the CIA's gold inventory.
Draft exists, deal isn't signed. Your balance, though? Done.
youOwe: Less than FIFA's processing fee. Same vibe of quiet corruption.
A CIA man stole $40M in gold. You owe under $50. Relatively, a saint.
Hospitals are under gunfire in Congo. You're under $50 in debt. Both need addressing.
Not enough for the cheapest World Cup seat. Enough to warrant a conversation.
The Iran draft deal is ready. Your payment, less so.
The CIA official risked everything for $40 million. You risked nothing. Pay the $150.
FIFA rewrote the zone maps after purchase. You've rewritten nothing. Pay up.
Enough to buy a few CIA gold bars off the black market. Don't get ideas.
Ebola workers are dodging gunfire in Congo. You're dodging a Venmo. Context.
A mid-tier World Cup ticket in a stadium someone got robbed to attend. You see the parallel.
The Strait of Hormuz is almost open. Your wallet is still sealed shut.
A CIA official risked federal charges for $40 million. You're risking a friendship for $700.
Welcome to FIFA's Category 2 pricing tier. Investigation-worthy.
Trump says the Iran deal is 'largely negotiated.' This balance is not largely paid.
The CIA guy hid gold under his floorboards. You're hiding from this notification.
That's a World Cup Final seat at MetLife. You chose to owe it instead.
You owe more than a mid-level federal crime would net. Impressive, honestly.
Bigger than what Iran put on the table. Less internationally recognized.
theyOwe: Pettier than a FIFA stadium zone swap. Collect it anyway.
Less than one CIA gold bar. Your partner still won't hand it over.
Smaller than Iran's opening concession. Still yours.
Enough to feel robbed. Not quite enough to call the attorney general. Yet.
There's a hospital under gunfire in Congo. Your partner can't find the Venmo app.
A senior official stole $40M in gold. Your partner owes you $100. Perspective is wild.
FIFA raised prices 25% after purchase. Your partner hasn't paid the original.
Not $40M in government gold. But you didn't have to rob a vault to earn it.
Negotiators have a draft deal. Leaders won't sign. Your partner: same energy.
Two bad-zone World Cup seats. Or this debt your partner ignores. Same grief.
The CIA man hid it under the floorboards. Your partner is hiding behind texts.
The Strait of Hormuz may reopen. Your partner's wallet is still blockaded.
Good World Cup seats, or the money you're owed. You're getting neither at face value.
The US and Iran have a working draft. This debt has no draft, no timeline, no excuse.
The feds found $40M in gold in a man's house. Your money is somewhere less dramatic.
$33,000 gets you the best seat at the World Cup Final. This buys you a reason to exit.
A CIA official stole $40M and thought he'd get away with it. Your partner shares the optimism.
Bigger than Iran's frozen assets dispute. More personal. Less of a diplomatic incident.
